channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Norwegian drama 'Dreams (Sex Love)' takes top prize at the Berlin Film Festival
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A Norwegian film about love, desire and self-discovery won top honors at the 75th Berlin International Film Festival on Saturday. A jury headed by American director Todd Haynes awarded the Golden Bear trophy to “Dreams (Sex Love)” by director Dag Johan Haugerud.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USDA scholarship for students at historically Black colleges suspended
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal scholarship aimed at boosting students from underserved and rural areas attending historically Black colleges and universities has been put on hold. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a long asthmatic respiratory crisis that required high flows of oxygen, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Thieves used a stolen card to buy a $523,000 lottery ticket. The victim wants to share the winnings
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Thieves used a stolen card to buy a winning French lottery ticket worth 500,000 euros ($523,000). But they vanished before cashing in —- and now they’re among France’s most famous fugitives. The man whose card was stolen, identified in police documents as Jean-David E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Fox 5

item.title: Food recalls increase, more deadly in 2024: Report
item.description: Hospitalizations and deaths caused by contaminated foods doubled in 2024, according to a new Public Interest Research Group report.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Family says SoCal boy died from dangerous social media challenge
item.description: A Southern California couple is mourning the death of their young son after they say he died from a dangerous social media challenge.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Two Virginia Beach officers dead after traffic stop Friday night
item.description: A traffic stop turned deadly Friday night after two Virginia officers were shot and killed, according to the Virginia Beach Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Paxton Coley, NewsNation

item.title: Universal reveals Shrek, SpongeBob lands for new theme park in Texas
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences recently revealed several themed areas that will be featured at Universal Kids Resort, a new family-focused theme park set to open next year in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KREX 5

item.title: Chaotic scene unfolded as 3 were shot and killed outside Kentucky driver's license office
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Witnesses described a chaotic scene in which three people were shot and killed outside of a Kentucky driver's license office, including one man shot multiple times by masked assailants.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida woman who survived parents' murders as toddler reveals 'missing piece' after killer's execution
item.description: A woman who survived her parents' murders as a toddler reveals her "missing piece" after their killer's execution and her childhood memories connected to the horrific crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is being buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group is scheduled to be buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ceiba Phillips, 11, couldn't believe what he saw when he returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after a wildfire reduced it to rubble. The ruins of his best friend’s house and his beloved school.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, Fox 5

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5
